title: Crispy Caramelized Kenyan Sweet Potato Bake
description: A funky, crispy, brunch-mood Kenyan dish featuring candied peel and wood-fired bake, made with ayurvedic sattvic-friendly ingredients. This sweet potato bake is perfect for a unique breakfast or brunch option.
instructions: Preheat the wood-fired oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the sliced sweet potatoes with coconut oil, ginger, cumin, turmeric, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the sweet potatoes out in a single layer on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the sweet potatoes in the wood-fired oven for 30 minutes, or until they are tender and lightly browned.
While the sweet potatoes are baking, prepare the candied orange peel by chopping it into small pieces.
After the sweet potatoes have baked for 30 minutes, remove them from the oven and sprinkle the chopped candied orange peel over the top.
Return the sweet potatoes to the oven and continue baking for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the orange peel is caramelized and crispy.
Remove the sweet potatoes from the oven and sprinkle with chopped fresh cilantro.
Serve the crispy caramelized Kenyan sweet potato bake hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
